title: What is your opinion? Can I go live ?
author: TopComplete9567
selftext:
Looking for feedback on this backtest.

Profit: +90.17%  
Max Equity DD: 11.06%  
Profit Factor: 1.59  
Sharpe Ratio: 17.57  
Trades: 134  
Win Rate: 44.03%

The strategy relies on a roughly 2:1 reward-to-risk ratio rather than a high win rate.

Would you consider these stats robust enough for live trading or a funded challenge?

comments.author: qqAzo
comments.body: A sharp of 17.57 - sounds completely wrong. With math like that I wouldn't trust any of the other numbers

replies.author: TopComplete9567
replies.body: I tried different months and years but sharp is always above 10

replies.author: qqAzo
replies.body:
Backtest it over a full 10 years and see if you get the same. I’ve personally never seen a sustainable sharp above 5. Is it 90% in a year? Aka CAGR. Is it 90% over 5 years? 

Personally I’d still argue the math is wrong on a 2:1 reward ratio with a 44% win rate.

replies.author: TopComplete9567
replies.body: Ok bro, I will backtest more and will update.Thank you❤️

comments.author: Big-Accident9701
comments.body: Yes you can. This looks very good

comments.author: BackTesting-Queen
comments.body: Impressive stats! A profit factor of 1.59 and a Sharpe ratio of 17.57 are certainly robust indicators of a potentially successful strategy. The 2:1 reward-to-risk ratio is a sound approach, as it's not always about having a high win rate, but rather about how well you manage your losses. Just remember, backtesting is only one part of the equation. It's crucial to also consider forward testing to validate your strategy in real-time market conditions. Tools like WealthLab can be quite handy for this. Lastly, always ensure your strategy aligns with your risk tolerance before going live or entering a funded challenge. Good luck!
